data.title: Telegram founder: French media reports that Telegram was forced to comply with European rules are false
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Telegram founder Pavel Durov posted on his personal channel: "French media reported that my detention in France last summer "finally" forced Telegram to comply with European rules. This is pure fabrication. Telegram has always complied with EU law. After August last year, the French police sent a request to Telegram in a legal manner as stipulated in the EU Digital Services Act. The French court therefore obtained the IP address of the suspect from Telegram, long before I was detained in France."

data.title: RFC multiple addresses simultaneously liquidated positions, causing panic, and the token price fell by more than 20% in a short period of time
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to on-chain data monitoring, multiple wallet addresses suspected to belong to the same group simultaneously liquidated RFC tokens, causing market panic. The price of RFC fell by more than 20% in a short period of time and is now reported at $0.067.

data.title: LBank has launched DiMA (DIMA)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, LBank has launched DiMA (DIMA) at 22:35 (SGT) on April 17, and opened the DIMA/USDT trading pair. Currently, the deposit service is open, and the withdrawal service will be open at 23:00 on April 18.

data.title: Blockworks Research: Hyperliquid’s average daily trading volume reached $6.4 billion in the past three months
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Blockworks Research released a report showing that Hyperliquid's average daily trading volume in the past three months can reach 6.4 billion US dollars, slightly higher than 50% of Bybit and OKX's daily trading volume. In contrast, Hyperliquid's closest competitor Jupiter Perps has an average daily trading volume of only 704 million US dollars, 88% lower than Hyperliquid. In addition, the report pointed out that Hyperliquid's core businesses are HyperCore (order book trading) and HyperEVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine Network), and HyperCore currently dominates. (Blockworks)

data.title: VanEck launches NODE exchange-traded fund to track cryptocurrency stocks
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News VanEck has been approved by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to launch the cryptocurrency stock tracking exchange-traded fund Onchain Economy ETF (NODE). The ETF will manage 30-60 stocks related to the digital asset field. Its investment portfolio may include cryptocurrency exchanges, miners, data centers, semiconductors, and up to 25% of cryptocurrency ETPs. It will start trading on May 14 and will indirectly invest in derivatives through an offshore subsidiary in the Cayman Islands. (CoinDesk)

data.title: LBank has launched BASEISFOREVERY1 U-margined perpetual contract
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, LBank has launched the BASEISFOREVERY1 U-based perpetual contract at 20:30 (SGT) on April 17, 2025, with a maximum leverage of 20 times.

data.title: Global Payments to acquire GTCR and FIS’s stake in Worldpay for $22.7 billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Payment technology and software solutions provider Global Payments Inc. announced a final agreement to sell its Issuer Solutions business to FIS for US$13.5 billion and acquire Worldpay from GTCR and FIS for a net price of US$22.7 billion, with a total value of US$24.25 billion. It is reported that the transaction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2026. (Businesswire)

data.title: Today, the US Bitcoin ETF has a net outflow of 2,050 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF has a net outflow of 10,484 ETH
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Lookonchain monitoring, 10 US Bitcoin ETFs had a net outflow of 2,050 BTC today, of which Fidelity had an outflow of 1,353 BTC. Fidelity currently holds 194,273 BTC, worth $16.33 billion; 9 Ethereum ETFs had a net outflow of 10,484 ETH, of which Grayscale ETHE had an outflow of 6,657 ETH. Grayscale ETHE currently holds 1,174,546 ETH, worth $1.86 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: According to Odaily, Lookonchain reports that today, ten U.S. Bitcoin ETFs saw a net outflow of 2,050 BTC. Among these, Fidelity experienced an outflow of 1,353 BTC, leaving it with a total holding of 194,273 BTC, valued at $16.33 billion. Additionally, nine Ethereum ETFs recorded a net outflow of 10,484 ETH. Grayscale ETHE accounted for 6,657 ETH of this outflow, with its current holdings at 1,174,546 ETH, valued at $1.86 billion.

data.title: Analyst: $75,000 may be the current defense area for Bitcoin bulls
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The market generally believes that any bearish trend caused by the unchanged Fed interest rate has been reflected in the Bitcoin price. Onchain analyst James Check pointed out that the real bottom of Bitcoin is at its real market average, that is, the average cost basis of active investors - about $65,000. The bears will try to defend the resistance level of $86,000, which increases the possibility of prices falling below $80,000 and hitting new lows. 
James Check added: "$75,000 may be the area where Bitcoin bulls defend in the current market. If the defense fails, the next step will be to return to the liquidation consolidation range to see how far it can go in the end." (Cointelegraph)
